title: Corniola scorpius
taxonomy: Corniola scorpius is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is sometimes referred to as the 'Scorpion's Tail' due to its unique appearance. The classification of Corniola scorpius includes being a member of the genus Corniola and the family Rutaceae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, including its flowers, leaves, and growth habits. The plant is also sometimes classified as Corniola scorpioides, however the exact classification can vary depending on the source.
uses: Corniola scorpius, a plant with various uses, has been known to have medicinal properties in traditional medicine.
